joke: I used to hate facial hair, but then it grew on me.
I asked my calendar for advice, and it said, “Your days are numbered.”
I tried to start a band with my vacuum cleaner, but it kept sucking.
I told my computer I needed a break, and now it keeps sending me vacation ads.
I started a bakery for cats, but it was a total cat-astrophe.
I tried to teach my dog algebra, but it kept chasing the x.
I got a job at a mirror factory. I can really see myself doing it.
I went to a psychic, but they charged me in advance.
I bought a telescope, but it just made my problems look farther away.
I told my bookshelf a secret, and now it has shelf-esteem.
I asked my sandwich for directions. It said, “Lettuce turn around.”
I tried to write a joke about pencils, but it had no point.
I asked my cactus for advice. It said, “Stick with it.”
I told a joke about gravity, but it really brought everyone down.
I opened a museum for left socks. It really needed a right direction.
I tried to organize a hide-and-seek tournament, but good luck finding a venue.
I joined a gym for introverts. Nobody wanted to work out in a crowd, so it was packed.
I bought a boomerang. Now I’m in a long-distance relationship with it.
I told a chemistry joke, but there was no reaction.
I told a joke about roads, but it just went nowhere.
I told a mathematician a joke about zero. It had no value.
I tried to catch fog yesterday. Mist.
I bought a ladder. It’s a step up in my life.
I got a pet octopus. It’s great at multitasking, but terrible at keeping secrets.
I asked a penguin for investment advice. It said, “Keep your portfolio cool.”
I auditioned for a silent film. They said I was too talkative.
I tried to become an astronaut, but my career never took off.
I told the ceiling a joke, and it just went over everyone’s head.
I bought a piano with one key missing. It was a big note-ably bad decision.
I tried to organize a surprise party for a lamp, but it just kept getting too bright.
